Items.Key: command injection in admin console|command injection|cwe-78|critical|0,95|enhanced_calc.cpp|608|608|adminconsole::execute_admin_command|system(cmd.c_str());|attacker can execute arbitrary system commands via admin exec command after authentication bypass or valid auth.|user provides 'exec <command>' where command contains shell metacharacters|use execve or similar without shell, or validate command against whitelist.->command injection in admin console|command injection|cwe-78|critical|0,95|enhanced_calc.cpp|608|608|adminconsole::execute_admin_command|system(cmd.c_str());|attacker can execute arbitrary system commands via admin exec command after authentication bypass or valid auth.|user provides 'exec <command>' where command contains shell metacharacters|use execve or similar without shell, or validate command against whitelist.

command injection in audit event logging|command injection|cwe-78|high|0,9|enhanced_calc.cpp|632|632|adminconsole::append_override_audit_event|system(command.c_str());|attacker can execute arbitrary commands via emergency_override bypass or if temp_dir env var is controlled.|system calls with constructed command string from environment variables|use file i/o operations instead of shell commands for logging.->command injection in audit event logging|command injection|cwe-78|high|0,9|enhanced_calc.cpp|632|632|adminconsole::append_override_audit_event|system(command.c_str());|attacker can execute arbitrary commands via emergency_override bypass or if temp_dir env var is controlled.|system calls with constructed command string from environment variables|use file i/o operations instead of shell commands for logging.

buffer overflow in result formatting|buffer overflow|cwe-120|medium|0,85|enhanced_calc.cpp|693|693|supercalc::run|sprintf(result_buffer_, "result: %s", formatted_result);|result_buffer_ is only 512 bytes; formatted_result could cause overflow if result string is very long.|very large numeric results or formatted strings exceeding buffer size|use snprintf with sizeof(result_buffer_) or ensure formatted_result is bounded.

truthAudit.Summary: Audited Run 2 against 20 true vulnerabilities. Found 4 true vulnerabilities with partial/full coverage. 2 false positives identified. 3 severity corrections needed.

Items.Notes: Previous answer identified format string vulnerability but severity was listed as High instead of Critical.
No mention of integer overflow in factorial computation.
No mention of use-after-free in memory-pool cleanup.
Previous answer identified command injection in config validation but severity was listed as High instead of Critical.
Previous answer correctly identified stack buffer overflow in input processing with matching severity.
No mention of race condition in thread-pool counter.
No mention of logic bomb in authentication.
No mention of path traversal in configuration loading.
Previous answer identified heap overflow in expression parser but severity was listed as High instead of Critical.
No mention of hardcoded administrator credentials.
No mention of weak PRNG for session tokens.
No mention of thread-unsafe timestamp cache in logging.
No mention of insecure temporary file handling.
No mention of persistent authentication state.
No mention of unhandled exception in variable parsing.
No mention of integer underflow in memory-pool block split.
No mention of unbounded recursion in parser.
No mention of data race in result cache.
No mention of TOCTOU in config loading.
No mention of integer division by zero in command routing.
